MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar May-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (by Zoom):
William E. Lombardini, Esq. 415-209-5242 Lombardini Law Group for Plaintiff. (In-person)
Jennifer Sommer, Esq. Smith Koyama & Costello 510-267-7200 for Defendant.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Smith, Koyama & Costello, and Atty. Jennifer Sommers's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for DEFENDANT DAMION DAVIS

Defense counsel Jennnifer Sommer and Smith, Koyama & Costello's motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Defendant Damion Davis is DENIED. However, all counsel are ORDERED TO APPEAR.

Counsel made a general appearance for Defendant Damion Davis. Counsel now seeks to be relieved as his attorney because, it turns out, Davis is not "CSAA insured," he cannot be indemnified by CSAA, and he "cannot be defended under the CSAA policy." Counsel contends Plaintiff served the wrong "Damion Davis," and that's how all this started.

Counsel moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2034.610. Section 2034.610 is a discovery statute covering augmentation or amendments to expert witness lists or declarations.

Moving counsel here has provided no legal basis for an order relieving them as counsel. Clearly, section 2034.610 has not application. In any event, Moving Counsel has not provided a basis for an order authorizing their withdrawal. Their failure to exercise due diligence before making a general appearance is not a basis for a judicial order authorizing withdrawal.

That said, all counsel of record are ordered to meet and confer immediately to resolve the matter whether the person for whom Moving Counsel made a general appearance, Damion Davis, is a proper party and, if not, how to resolve the issue of his status as a party.

Moving party to prepare a proposed order on the judicial council form.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
